text: Hey there everyone, my name is Zitesh and before we move on into this course, I would like to take small amount of time of yours to make sure that I share something very personal to you and since this is a mobile development entire course, I would love to share my journey of mobile development as well. See, my name is Hitesh and I've been writing code for almost more than 15 years probably I guess. I haven't counted but it's almost like that. And I was never a programmer in the initial days. I started my journey as a penetration tester and cyber security. So security was all the fun that I enjoyed. And to be very honest, in those days I didn't like programming that much. I was very happy in cybersecurity ecosystem. But then one day I found out about mobile application development. And I instantly fell in love with programming. The amount of programming that I truly started to learn, I knew programming before that as well, bits and pieces, but the real world implementation that I truly learned was through the mobile development itself. For the very first time I felt that I not only can create things but actually I can create something very personalized which lives in my phone and I can carry it day in and day out. After that I built so many application to help my dad for me personally. A lot of them were very naive but it was very personal to me. that first thing helped me to fall in love with programming. So mobile development ecosystem will always get a special place in my heart because this is so close to me. This is where first time I saw that the huge potential as well as love of building something and now we are in the age of AI. It doesn't matter whether AI is helping you to know the concept and write it faster and build your product faster or you're writing the entire code on your own doesn't really matter. What matters is that you can build something very personal for yourself or some company or somebody in the family or friend and They can keep this entire thing with them in their personal phone is a very personal device And not only that once I fell into love of building so many of these applications Also, I noticed that there's a huge demand in mobile application ecosystem but not enough people who are taking interest in it and I saw that a lot of people learn different kind of things, maybe machine learning, maybe AI, maybe backend engineering or maybe web development but chances are high, here is the small test, that if you're watching a video on the YouTube, chances are high that you majorly watch it on a mobile app. If you're watching it on Udemy, chances are high that you're watching it on the mobile app as well. So every single application which is going viral needs a mobile app and I was always wondering why there are not enough mobile engineers or mobile developers who can actually take those roles. Every single company always struggles in hiring those engineers because there are very few of them and that's what happened early in my career as well. I started to building mobile apps, I was enjoying it and then I saw opportunities coming to my way. I build so many applications remotely for the US client, for Japanese client, for Vietnamese client and all of that right sitting at my home and I was able to charge whatever I wish for because there are not enough bids on those freelancing projects. There's nobody who can actually build an end-to-end mobile application and serve it to the people. And when there is a demand which is low, you can definitely charge. Now, nowadays, the demand is definitely going a little on the higher side, but still, compared to web development or machine learning or any other profession, mobile development is still an untapped territory where not many people are there to tap into. So... My humble request is take this very very seriously. This course is all that you need
